Patch Set 2: Published edit on patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

I attempted to fix the build errors present in this patch and succeeded. Yay! Now, keep in mind I barely have any idea what I am doing, so please doublecheck the changes I made to make sure they make sense.

This service is only provided in hopes to be useful and reduce latency from push to getting actual results in the face of trivial code errors

Here's summary of my changes:
Excellent! The fix has been applied successfully.

## Summary

**The Fix:**
Changed `res->lqe_glbl_data` to `lqe->lqe_glbl_data` on line 231 of `lustre/quota/qmt_lock.c`.

**Rationale:**
The original patch attempted to change the error message to print `lqe_glbl_data` instead of `lr_lvb_data`, which makes sense given the context (the code is checking if `lqe->lqe_glbl_data` is held by the work queue). However, the patch incorrectly tried to access this field from the `res` variable (which is a `struct ldlm_resource`), when it should have accessed it from the `lqe` variable (which is a `struct lquota_entry`).

The `struct ldlm_resource` only has the field `lr_lvb_data`, while `struct lquota_entry` has the field `lqe_glbl_data`. Since the code is checking `lqe->lqe_glbl_data` on line 220 and the error message is meant to print the pointer value of this field when it's unexpectedly held, the correct fix is to use `lqe->lqe_glbl_data` instead of `res->lqe_glbl_data`.

parents.subject: LU-19537 quota: fix race of lqe_glbl_lgd and qmt_sarr

commit.subject: LU-19537 quota: Fix error print
commit.message:
LU-19537 quota: Fix error print

When we cannot get the lvb because it's held by something else, print
lqe_glbl_data because that's the important bit,
the lr_lvb_data is always 0 at that point.

Change-Id: Iedc4f629afa04948060f089fcc6952204dcedb5b
Fixes: c606081b062a ("LU-19537 quota: fix race of lqe_glbl_lgd and qmt_sarr")
Signed-off-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
